stories.title: US senator wants urgent answers from Barclays over Jes Staley’s ties to Jeffrey Epstein
stories.summary: Exclusive: Elizabeth Warren says it is unclear how bank failed to uncover former CEO’s links with sex offender The leading US senator Elizabeth Warren has called for urgent answers from Barclays over what she claimed was its “apparent failure to meaningfully investigate” ties between the bank’s former boss, Jes Staley, and the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. In a letter sent privately to Barclays’s chair, Nigel Higgins, and seen by the Guardian, Warren – the most senior Democrat on the US sen

stories.title: Carmakers lobbied UK to revoke ban on petrol and diesel cars after 2035
stories.summary: BMW, Ford, Nissan and Toyota, plus parts maker Bosch, wrote joint letter to ministers in April Major carmakers privately lobbied the UK government to revoke a ban on new petrol and diesel cars after 2035, according to documents that reveal for the first time the efforts to overturn a key decarbonisation policy. BMW, Ford, Nissan and Toyota, plus the parts maker Bosch, wrote a joint letter to ministers in April calling for an “open technology approach” including petrol and diesel cars after 2035,

stories.title: Hungary: Ex-PM Orban pledges resistance to Magyar 'tyranny'
stories.summary: Hungary's former prime minister alleges that his successor has "dismantled the rule of law and eliminated checks and balances." Peter Magyar has pursued Viktor Orban and his allies since winning April's elections.

stories.title: Meglepő fordulat: mégis átveheti az állam Balásy Gyula cégeit
stories.summary: Bár Magyar Péter azt mondta, hogy nem veszik át Balásy Gyula vállalatait, most mégis átvilágítják a cégeket, és kedvező eredmény esetén vagyonuk az államhoz kerülhet.

stories.title: Becsületsértés gyanúja merült fel a Magyar Pétert leköpő férfi ügyében
stories.summary: Ugyan a miniszterelnök nem tett feljelentést, de mások megtették.

stories.title: A kórházban a pelenkáját többszöri kérésünk ellenére is csak órákkal később cserélték ki
stories.summary: Nagy port kavart egy ismert pszichiáter nyilvános Facebook-posztja, amiben egy budapesti kórházban szerzett negatív tapasztalatait osztotta meg. De vajon hogy lehet, hogy ugyanez a beteg egy másik kórházban rendes ellátást kapott? A nővérek nincsenek könnyű helyzetben, de a megfelelő kommunikáció nem csak pénzkérdés, sok múlik a helyi szervezeti kultúrán is.

stories.title: Olyan trógerül használják a budapestiek a szelektív gyűjtőszigeteket, hogy a kerületek sorra számolják fel őket
stories.summary: Sok köztéri gyűjtőnél ad hoc lomtalanítási pont alakul ki, de még az elvileg oda való szemetet sem képesek normálisan kidobni az emberek. Az illegális hulladéklerakás hatalmas suttyóság, de nem csak egyéni felelősség, a jelenlegi rendszerben viszont esély sincs a fejlődésre.

stories.title: Évekig ömlöttek a magyar milliárdok, a Tisza-kormány most felülvizsgálja az erdélyi focitámogatásokat
stories.summary: A magyar kormány egyelőre nem árulta el, milyen jövőt szán a határon túli futball támogatásának, de a Transtelex kérdésére először közölte a jelenlegi támogatási összegeket, és azt is megerősítette, hogy felülvizsgálják a programot. Tusványoson eközben a Sepsi OSK és az FK Csíkszereda vezetői már arról beszéltek, hogyan készülnek arra az esetre, ha kevesebb pénz érkezik Magyarországról. A Sepsi OSK és az FK Csíkszereda az elmúlt négy évben több mint 20 milliárd forintnyi magyarországi forrást kö

stories.title: Trump defends 50% tariffs on Canada, saying ‘they need us to survive’
stories.summary: Mark Carney says Canada is ‘united’ in face of threats as leaders agree to talk before tariffs take effect next month Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Donald Trump has defended his new 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, claiming the country cannot survive without the US. The White House announced the sweeping round of import duties – due to come into effect next month – on Monday, igniting fresh tensions between Washington and Ottawa. Continue reading...

stories.title: My friends left Ukraine after the Russian invasion. Now I wonder if we live in the same world | Diana Berg
stories.summary: Anger, guilt, shame: the deepest unseen wound of this war is the rift between those who left and those who stayed behind “Can we reschedule?” Over the years of working with western colleagues, I had learned to accept that they don’t fully understand what war feels like from the inside. They would ask me to find someone in occupied Mariupol for an interview, or seem genuinely puzzled that I couldn’t just log on at a time that suited them. I made my peace with this long ago – they don’t have to un

stories.title: Akik éltek a Kádár-korszakban, aligha gondolták, hogy nem is olyan sokára egy hasonló rendszer épül ki
stories.summary: Kovács Krisztián íróval a magyar űrkutatás kezdeteiről, az ügynökakták nyilvánosságra hozataláról és a szembenézés fontosságáról beszélgettünk A fény magányossága című regénye apropóján. Interjú.

stories.summary: A Barcelona játékosa szerint Paredest még a meccsen kellett volna kiállítani, nem pedig utána.

stories.summary: Eközben a Pécsi Nemzeti színház bejelentette, hogy nem viszik színpadra Orbán A Mester és Margaritáját.

stories.title: Puszta kézzel küzdött egy utas életéért egy magyar orvos a fedélzeten, a Ryanair szerint minden járaton van defibrillátor
stories.summary: Egy magyar kardiológus a kisgyerekével együtt utazott a légitársaság Madrid–Budapest járatán, amikor egy férfi rosszul lett. Az utast egy idő után újra kellett éleszteni. A légiutas-kísérők azt mondták az orvosnak, hogy semmilyen orvosi műszer nincs a fedélzeten. A Ryanair viszont azt írta a Telexnek, hogy minden gépükön van defibrillátor, de azt nem kérte az orvos.

stories.title: Why a 90-year-old treaty limits NATO in the Black Sea
stories.summary: A 90-year-old treaty still shapes the balance of power in the Black Sea. As Ukraine fights on against Russia, can NATO project power into waters it can't enter freely?

stories.summary: E-commerce group in Russia calls for tax relief while Ukraine maintains its targets serve as suppliers to Moscow’s military. stories.summary: Prime minister says ‘those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared’ as exam leaks scandal spirals into widespread anger with his government Narendra Modi has said he will set up “fast track courts” to expedite the punishment of corrupt officials who leaked examination papers,as India’s prime minister seeks to quell the youth-led “cockroach” protest movement that has rocked Delhi and challenged his government. In his first public remarks on the movement, Modi said on X “Nothing

stories.title: Trump’s air war in Iran may be reaching its limits
stories.summary: President Donald Trump’s latest threat to bomb Iranian infrastructure is unlikely to rattle Tehran — and instead underscores the limits of what an air war alone can accomplish. The Trump administration has become increasingly frustrated by Iran’s refusal to capitulate, as the two sides have escalated attacks over the past month in a fight for control of the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway for world energy supplies. Now, Trump has pledged to hit “one bridge or power plant” for eve

stories.title: Trump administration announces deal to export nuclear materials to Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The Trump administration announced a major civil nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia on Wednesday, after decades of on-and-off conversations between the country and the Obama, Biden and Trump White Houses. The Department of Energy confirmed in a press release that Energy Secretary Chris Wright and Saudi Minister of Energy Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman signed two agreements that would allow private American nuclear companies to export to the Saudi market. stories.title: OpenAI’s models broke free and launched a cyberattack. US Congress wants new rules before it happens again.
stories.summary: An alarming cybersecurity incident involving OpenAI’s newest and most advanced artificial intelligence technology has lawmakers hurrying to advance legislation that they say is needed to prevent AI from spinning dangerously outside human control. stories.summary: Criticism comes as prime minister frames unity as point of difference to political opponents, saying ALP chooses ‘progress over protest’ Outspoken Labor MP Ed Husic claims the party has become “allergic” to dissent and dispute, warning the unity on show at its tightly controlled national conference could backfire. stories.summary: Brussels talks between senior diplomats now underway in attempt to sign off 21st package of sanctions, after previous failed attempts The Venice Biennale said it would challenge a European Union decision to withdraw a €2 ⁠million grant in response to the return of Russia to its 2026 edition, despite continuing aggression on Ukraine, Reuters reported. stories.title: Iran warns of ‘eye for an eye’ response if US follows through on Trump’s threats to destroy infrastructure – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. The Jordanian army said it intercepted four Iranian missiles on Wednesday with two additional missile falling in an open area, AFP reports. The two missiles that the army did not intercept fell in “two remote, uninhabited areas, without causing any human casualties or material damage” the army said in a statement. Continue reading...

stories.title: Police officer fatally shoots man in Madison, Wisconsin, sparking protests
stories.summary: Shooting happened on street in popular neighborhood lined with restaurants, bars, shops and homes A police officer in Madison, Wisconsin, shot and killed a man Wednesday in an incident that was captured on cellphone footage, sparking local protests . John Patterson, the Madison police chief, acknowledged the shooting at a news conference, but stressed footage circulating was just one perspective of what happened. Continue reading...

stories.title: Nobody in Europe knows or cares what Kemi Badenoch thinks. That’s a big mistake | Simon Nixon
stories.summary: The opposition leader wants to pull the UK out of the ECHR, wrecking treaties from the Good Friday agreement to the Gibraltar deal Sitting in Brussels, or one of the EU’s 27 chancelleries, one could be forgiven for tuning out the latest psychodramas in Britain’s Conservative party. stories.title: Scottish police officer was rapist who attacked women over 14 years
stories.summary: Alan Greer, who killed himself after officers visited his home, could be ‘Police Scotland’s Wayne Couzens moment’ A police officer who took his own life in May was a rapist who attacked at least nine women over 14 years. PC Alan Greer’s offending came to light when his first known victim, a woman he raped in her home in Glasgow in 2012 after she had called the police to report being the victim of a crime, reported the attack late last year. stories.title: Opposition parties shut down Indian parliament in solidarity with ‘cockroach’ protests
stories.summary: Proceedings suspended as politicians call for Modi government to address police violence and education scandals India’s opposition parties have shut down parliament in a protest calling for the resignation of the education minister, in solidarity with the new youth-led “cockroach” movement . Opposition politicians, led by the Congress party leader Rahul Gandhi, gathered outside the parliament building in Delhi on Wednesday, many dressed in black and waving placards calling on Dharmendra Pradhan

stories.title: Zelenskyy ousts Ukraine army chief after protests - The Latest
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired his commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, in Ukraine’s biggest military shake-up in two years. Nationwide protests broke out after Zelenskyy sacked Mykhailo Fedorov as defence minister, who the Ukraine president says will now be brought back into government in a ‘prominent position’. Lucy Hough speaks to the Guardian’s senior international correspondent Luke Harding – watch on YouTube Continue reading...

stories.title: Zelenskyy under growing pressure to reinstate sacked defence minister
stories.summary: Campaigners in Ukraine pledge to continue nationwide protests until Mykhailo Fedorov gets his job back Volodymyr Zelenskyy was under growing pressure to reinstate his sacked defence minister on Wednesday, after campaigners said they would continue indefinite nationwide protests until Mykhailo Fedorov got his job back. On Tuesday, Zelenskyy bowed to public demands by firing Ukraine’s commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi . He appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new head of the country’s armed force

stories.title: US and Saudi Arabia reach historic nuclear energy deal
stories.summary: The deal is another sign of growing US-Saudi ties during US President Donald Trump's second term. The agreement, known as a 123 Agreement, would allow Saudi Arabia to build nuclear reactors using US technology.

stories.title: Iran war: Trump threatens bridges over Hormuz Strait attacks
stories.summary: US President Trump has threatened to destroy a "bridge or power plant" for each ship fired upon in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's foreign minister said any attack would "compel a powerful and decisive response."

stories.title: US hits Iran as Houthis claim strikes on Saudi tankers
stories.summary: The US is carrying out its 12th night of strikes on Iran. Meanwhile, Yemen's Houthis are claiming attacks on Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea. DW has the latest.

stories.title: India: Modi orders fast-track courts for paper leaks amid protests
stories.summary: India's PM Narendra Modi has announced speedy trials to punish those involved in paper leaks, without directly commenting on the ongoing student protests across the nation. DW has more.

stories.title: Mykhailo Drapatyi: Who is Ukraine's new commander-in-chief?
stories.summary: Ukraine has a new army chief, with Mykhailo Drapatyi replacing Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief. The change follows a cabinet reshuffle that ousted the popular defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov.

stories.summary: The US president said Washington would destroy one piece of civilian infrastructure — including sites near Tehran — each time Iran fires on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, as the war entered its 11th consecutive day of US strikes.

stories.title: EU countries clinch deal on Russia sanctions
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU countries reached a deal Thursday on a sweeping 21st sanctions package against Russia. The final sticking point — a proposed ban on shipping Russian gas to third countries — was resolved through a limited exemption for contracts signed before Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, while prohibiting EU operators from entering into new ones, two EU diplomats said, speaking anonymously to discuss the agreement. The bloc also agreed to freeze its price cap on Russian crude oil for 12

stories.title: Moscow-linked nuclear project in northwest Germany gets green light
stories.summary: BERLIN — A nuclear project linked to Russian state-owned company Rosatom has been given the go-ahead by state authorities in northwest Germany, under strict conditions to mitigate risks posed by Russian involvement. As first reported by POLITICO , the Lower Saxony Ministry of the Environment granted French company Framatome permission on Wednesday to manufacture Russian-designed nuclear fuel rods and assemblies in the town of Lingen, northwest Germany. stories.summary: Analysis shows how heatwaves are making atmosphere thirstier, damaging water supplies, food production and power generation Extreme heat supercharged by the climate crisis is sucking Europe dry in summer and intensifying the drought hitting the continent, a scientific analysis has found. stories.title: Total solar eclipse books out hotels two years in advance in New Zealand city
stories.summary: Dunedin will be the focal point, having last experienced such an eclipse in 1163 – before any humans reached New Zealand Hotels are booking out two years in advance in the New Zealand city of Dunedin as it prepares for the region’s first total solar eclipse in more than 850 years. At 4.17pm on 22 July 2028, the moon will pass in front of the sun, casting a roughly 100km-wide shadowy band across parts of southern New Zealand. stories.title: Michelin-starred restaurant owner in South Korea faces jail over ant dessert

stories.title: France: Minister quits over pesticide bill — but is staying

In one hand was an anti-government poster and in the other was a bicycle helmet. “We need to have protection in case Delhi police beat us up again,” said the 25-year-old, who stood among her friends. “We are not scared of them and we won’t run away, no matter what they do. We’ll stay here till they listen.” On Tuesday, central Delhi st

stories.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
stories.summary: Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby. Researchers from Project Ceti (the Cetacean Translation Initiative), a non-profit research organisation working to decode

stories.title: Nicaragua’s congress announces ‘work plan’ to suspend elections
stories.summary: US secretary of state says international community should ‘join forces’ against authoritarian Ortega regime Nicaragua’s congress, controlled by the country’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has announced a “work plan” to implement the authoritarian leader’s order that the country should no longer hold elections. The UN and the US strongly condemned Sunday’s announcement by Ortega, who has been in power since 2007 and, after a controversial constitutional change last year, now governs alon

stories.title: France records more than 5,700 excess deaths during June heatwave
stories.summary: Figures come as large parts of southern Europe endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperatures France recorded more than 5,700 excess deaths during its June heatwave, authorities have said, as swathes of southern Europe continue to endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperatures. The French national health agency said on Wednesday that it had recorded 5,764 excess deaths ⁠between ​17 June and 2 July, an excess mortality rate of 36%. Continue reading...

stories.title: Police station opens in Hitler's birth house

stories.title: Andy Burnham’s plans could bring the UK closer to Europe than he realises
stories.summary: The new PM’s priorities are domestic, but he will soon realise that closer engagement with the EU is a no-brainer What will European leaders make of Andy Burnham, the seventh UK prime minister they have had to deal with in a decade of chronic political instability in London unleashed by the fateful 2016 referendum vote to leave the European Union? The “king of the north” is largely an unknown quantity in continental Europe and has given priority to a domestic agenda of devolving powers to region

stories.title: What the EU wants from Andy Burnham

Continue reading...

stories.summary: German rapper Danger Dan and pianist Igor Levitt had been invited to play a song for the 100th episode of a political satire show on TV. The performance was canceled at the last minute due to its message.

stories.summary: Firefighters battle a major wildfire in Spain's Guadalajara province as flames destroy thousands of hectares and force more than 1,200 people to flee.

stories.title: Russian prison intercepts cat used to smuggle drugs into penal colony
stories.summary: In Russia, offenders reportedly used a cat to smuggle illegal drugs into Correctional Colony No. 17 in the city of Nizhny Novgorod.

stories.title: US appeals court rejects Biden bid to block release of memoir ghostwriter recordings
stories.summary: A divided three-judge panel of the US Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit suspended its decision until 3 August to give Biden more time to consider a further appeal.

stories.title: Aliyev says former Russian and German officials were in Baku for possible meeting over Ukraine
stories.summary: Azerbaijan’s president revealed that former Russian and German officials were in Baku at the same time earlier in July, but that Azerbaijan’s authorities were neither notified nor aware of the meeting, which might have involved discussions over Ukraine.

stories.title: Iran war has cost US $37.5bn so far, Hegseth says, as Pentagon seeks billions more
stories.summary: The hearing was disrupted by protests and a shouting match as Hegseth testified at a pivotal time in the war.

stories.title: Trump says US has no interest in Iran talks as cost of war increases
stories.summary: The US entered its 11th consecutive night of strikes on Iran, while Tehran expanded its offensive against Gulf states. Meanwhile, Pakistan stepped up diplomatic efforts in hopes of salvaging the interim deal between the two.

stories.title: Outcry in Algeria over doctor deaths after gruelling on-call shifts
stories.summary: A resident neurosurgeon died of a heart attack after a 24-hour shift, and an anaesthesiologist died days later — deaths colleagues say reflect years of chronic overwork, understaffing and inadequate conditions in Algeria's public hospitals, demanding a revision of on-call rules dating back to 2013.

stories.title: Watch: Beluga whales travel by plane and lorry to US from closed Canadian marine park
stories.summary: Six beluga whales have arrived in Illinois and Texas after being moved from a Canadian marine park that could no longer care for them.

stories.title: Aerial footage reveals the scale of Spain’s La Mierla wildfire
stories.summary: Aerial footage shows the scale of the La Mierla wildfire in Guadalajara, Spain, as firefighters battle a blaze that has burned up to 30,000 hectares.

stories.title: Mamdani says NYC lacks authority to arrest Israeli PM Netanyahu
stories.summary: Netanyahu's office has dubbed the ICC a "kangaroo court" and called the arrest warrant against the Israeli leader "bogus."

stories.title: Germany news: Merz hosts Azerbaijan President Aliyev
stories.summary: Talks between the German chancellor and Ilham Aliyev have focused on energy. Meanwhile, Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt will present a report on sexual offenses against children.

stories.title: Tanker attacked in Strait of Hormuz as US strikes Iran for tenth consecutive night
stories.summary: As fighting escalates between both sides, Iran's interior minister travelled to Pakistan, a key mediator in the conflict, for talks.

stories.title: Germany news: Resistance to Nazi terror commemorated
stories.summary: Monday marks 82 years since "Operation Valkyrie," the failed attempt on Adolf Hitler's life in 1944. Meanwhile, Foreign Minister Wadephul is heading to Africa on official business.

stories.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's new prime minister
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has been sworn in as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Burnham, the former Manchester mayor, has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

stories.title: UN chief Guterres to visit Cyprus in bid to revive peace talks
stories.summary: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres will visit Cyprus next week in what is seen as an effort to kickstart the stalled peace process. Why now? What is known about the plan? And what might be different this time round?

stories.title: AliExpress hit with record EU fine
stories.summary: The Chinese retailer has become the latest major online marketplace to face penalties under the EU’s Digital Services Act.

stories.title: How heat waves are messing with your sleep
stories.summary: Residents across Europe have struggled to sleep during the latest record heat waves. But as the planet continues to warm, sleep deprivation could create an epidemic of mental and physical ill-health.

stories.title: Ukraine fires 400 drones at Moscow as Russia steps up ballistic missile attacks
stories.summary: Ukraine's latest attack follows a weekend of escalating exchanges between the two sides, with Russia targeting Kyiv with ballistic missiles on Sunday and Ukraine hitting warehouses outside of Moscow on Saturday.

stories.title: High divers take the plunge at Moscow's Two Rivers festival
stories.summary: High divers from 11 countries compete at Moscow's Two Rivers festival, featuring river jumps, rowing races and hydroflight performances.

stories.title: IRGC pledges 'unforgettable lesson' for US as Iran signals return to talks
stories.summary: The Revolutionary Guard told Ayatollah Khamenei it regarded his message as "a binding command," as Iran's foreign ministry said diplomacy remained open and mediators sought a 10-day ceasefire to revive the framework deal.

stories.title: Yemen’s Houthis threaten Bab el-Mandeb closure in embargo on Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The Iran-backed Houthis declared a maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia after accusing the kingdom of attacking Sana'a airport and maintaining a years-long blockade, putting one of the world's busiest shipping lanes at risk.

stories.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods citing disputes over autos
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.

stories.title: China-Kazakhstan cooperation offers a qualitative shift
stories.summary: Beijing and Astana just signed a cooperation plan until 2030. In it and in the accompanying business agreements, analysts found signs of moving beyond traditional trade, as Kazakhstan presented itself as a full-cycle economic hub rather than a raw-material supplier.

stories.title: Victorious Spanish team parade through Madrid after World Cup triumph
stories.summary: Spain's World Cup-winning team toured Madrid on an open-top bus as huge crowds lined the streets to celebrate their victory over Argentina

stories.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
stories.summary: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in

stories.title: Russian army deserters in Germany: 'No choice but to run'
stories.summary: Almost all asylum applications by Russian deserters are being denied by German authorities. DW and Russian-language media outlet Astra spoke to one of the young men who fears he'll be sent back to die on the front lines.

stories.title: Chaos erupts in Buenos Aires after Argentina's World Cup final defeat
stories.summary: Riot police use water cannon in Buenos Aires after Argentina's World Cup final defeat to Spain, as protests break out in the capital.

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Israeli airstrikes in Gaza, wildfires in Europe, ICE in Maine and the World Cup semi-finals – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: New footage shows assassination attempt on Ukrainian businessman in Monaco – video
stories.summary: Ukraine's prosecutor general, Ruslan Kravchenko, said investigators have recovered key evidence in the case of the attempted assassination of the Ukrainian businessman Vadym Iermolaiev in Monaco. Specialists from Ukraine's security services restored a surveillance-camera recording that suspects had allegedly attempted to destroy. According to the prosecutor general, the surveillance camera had been installed near the crime scene to obtain confirmation that the alleged contract killing had been c

stories.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
stories.summary: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

stories.title: Why did Jens Spahn's surrogacy spark debate in Germany?
stories.summary: Jens Spahn, a key ally of Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is stepping down amid a controversy surrounding his decision to have a child via a surrogate mother. He had opposed legalizing surrogacy in Germany.
